1422	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — properness scaffold (`scaffold` GrassmannianCells.lean)
1423	
1424	Following Nitsure §1, "Properness" (`lem:gr_proper`): the structure morphism
1425	`toSpecZ : Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is **proper**. We discharge proper-ness through the Mathlib
1426	valuative criterion `IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion`, which reduces `IsProper toSpecZ` to four
1427	ingredients over `toSpecZ`:
1428	* `QuasiCompact` — from the finite affine chart cover (`compactSpace_scheme`);
1429	* `QuasiSeparated` — free from `isSeparatedToSpecZ` (`[IsSeparated f] : QuasiSeparated f`);
1430	* `LocallyOfFiniteType` — each chart map `ℤ → R^I = ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]` is of finite type;
1431	* `ValuativeCriterion` = `Existence ⊓ Uniqueness`, with `Uniqueness` free from separatedness
1432	  (`IsSeparated.valuativeCriterion`) and `Existence` the genuine Nitsure chart-selection content.
1433	
1434	This section builds the three "cheap" ingredients axiom-clean and isolates the existence
1435	obligation; see the task-result handoff for the precise decomposition of the existence part.
1436	
1437	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_proper`
1438	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1439	
1440	/-- The Grassmannian scheme is quasi-compact: it is glued from the finitely many
1441	(`Nat.choose r d`) affine charts `U^I`, each `Spec` of a ring (hence compact).
1442	Project-local: feeds `QuasiCompact (toSpecZ d r)` for properness. -/
1443	instance compactSpace_scheme (d r : ℕ) : CompactSpace (scheme d r) := by
1444	  haveI : Finite (theGlueData d r).openCover.I₀ :=
1445	    inferInstanceAs (Finite {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d})
1446	  haveI : ∀ i, CompactSpace ((theGlueData d r).openCover.X i) := fun i =>
1447	    inferInstanceAs (CompactSpace
1448	      (Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ i.1}) ℤ))))
1449	  exact (theGlueData d r).openCover.compactSpace
1450	
1451	/-- The structure morphism `Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is **quasi-compact**: `Spec ℤ` is affine and the
1452	Grassmannian scheme is a compact space (`compactSpace_scheme`). Project-local: the `QuasiCompact`
1453	input to the valuative criterion for properness. -/
1454	theorem quasiCompact_toSpecZ (d r : ℕ) : QuasiCompact (toSpecZ d r) := by
1455	  have : CompactSpace (scheme d r) := compactSpace_scheme d r
1456	  exact HasAffineProperty.iff_of_isAffine.mpr this
1457	
1458	/-- The structure morphism `Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is **locally of finite type**: on each chart the
1459	composite `U^I → Gr → Spec ℤ` is `Spec.map` of the structure map `ℤ → R^I = ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]`, a
1460	finitely generated `ℤ`-algebra. Project-local: the `LocallyOfFiniteType` input to the valuative
1461	criterion for properness. -/
1462	theorem locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ (d r : ℕ) : LocallyOfFiniteType (toSpecZ d r) := by
1463	  apply IsZariskiLocalAtSource.of_openCover (theGlueData d r).openCover
1464	  intro i
1465	  rw [show (theGlueData d r).openCover.f i ≫ toSpecZ d r
1466	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1467	          (algebraMap ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ i.1}) ℤ))) from ι_toSpecZ d r i]
1468	  exact (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFiniteType)).mpr
1469	    (RingHom.finiteType_algebraMap.mpr inferInstance)
1470	
1471	/-- The structure morphism `Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is **quasi-separated**: this is free from
1472	separatedness (`isSeparatedToSpecZ`), as every separated morphism is quasi-separated.
1473	Project-local: the `QuasiSeparated` input to the valuative criterion for properness. -/
1474	theorem quasiSeparated_toSpecZ (d r : ℕ) : QuasiSeparated (toSpecZ d r) := by
1475	  haveI : IsSeparated (toSpecZ d r) := isSeparatedToSpecZ d r
1476	  infer_instance
1477	
1478	/-- The **uniqueness part** of the valuative criterion for `toSpecZ` is free from separatedness:
1479	`IsSeparated.valuativeCriterion` says every separated morphism satisfies the uniqueness part
1480	(two `Spec R`-lifts agreeing on the generic point `Spec K` coincide). Project-local: the
1481	`Uniqueness` half of `ValuativeCriterion (toSpecZ d r)`. -/
1482	theorem valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ (d r : ℕ) :
1483	    ValuativeCriterion.Uniqueness (toSpecZ d r) := by
1484	  haveI : IsSeparated (toSpecZ d r) := isSeparatedToSpecZ d r
1485	  exact IsSeparated.valuativeCriterion (toSpecZ d r)
1486	
1487	/-- **The minor-ratio identity** `θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_{K'}) · P^I_J = P^I_{K'}` over `R^I_J`
1488	(generalises `transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul`, the `K' = I` case where `P^I_I = 1`): the
1489	pre-localisation transition hom sends the `K'`-minor of `X^J` to `det((X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_{K'}) =
1490	P^I_{K'} / P^I_J`. This is the algebraic core of the valuative-criterion existence argument
1491	(Nitsure §1): pulling back through a `K`-point `f : R^I → K` it yields
1492	`g(P^J_{K'}) = f(P^I_{K'}) / f(P^I_J)`, whose valuation `≥ 0` (Nitsure additive) / `≤ 1`
1493	(multiplicative) by minimality drives the entries of `g(X^J)` into the valuation ring.
1494	Project-local: feeds the existence half of `ValuativeCriterion (toSpecZ d r)`. -/
1495	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1496	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1497	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) *
1498	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1499	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
1500	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1501	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by
1502	  rw [transitionPreMap_minorDet]
1503	  have hsub : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
1504	        (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))
1505	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
1506	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
1507	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
1508	  rw [hsub, Matrix.det_mul]
1509	  have hdet : (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
1510	          (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).submatrix id
1511	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det
1512	        = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by
1513	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_map]; exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1514	  rw [hdet]
1515	  have hu : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
1516	      = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
1517	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1518	  have hone : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det *
1519	      algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1520	    rw [← hu, ← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
1521	  rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm _ (algebraMap _ _ (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)), ← mul_assoc, hone, one_mul]
1522	
1523	/-- **Properness reduced to the existence part of the valuative criterion** (`lem:gr_proper`,
1524	reduction form): the structure morphism `toSpecZ : Gr(d,r) → Spec ℤ` is proper as soon as the
1525	*existence* part of the valuative criterion holds. The three "cheap" ingredients —
1526	`QuasiCompact` (`quasiCompact_toSpecZ`), `QuasiSeparated` (`quasiSeparated_toSpecZ`),
1527	`LocallyOfFiniteType` (`locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ`) — and the *uniqueness* part
1528	(`valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ`, free from separatedness) are discharged here, so the only
1529	remaining obligation for `Grassmannian.isProper` is the Nitsure chart-selection existence
1530	statement `ValuativeCriterion.Existence (toSpecZ d r)`. Project-local. -/
1531	theorem isProper_of_valuativeExistence (d r : ℕ)
1532	    (hE : ValuativeCriterion.Existence (toSpecZ d r)) : IsProper (toSpecZ d r) := by
1533	  haveI : QuasiCompact (toSpecZ d r) := quasiCompact_toSpecZ d r
1534	  haveI : QuasiSeparated (toSpecZ d r) := quasiSeparated_toSpecZ d r
1535	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (toSpecZ d r) := locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ d r
1536	  apply IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion
1537	  exact ValuativeCriterion.iff.mpr ⟨hE, valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ d r⟩
1538	
1539	/-! ## Existence step E1 — chart selection (`scaffold` GrassmannianCells.lean)
1540	
1541	The existence half of the valuative criterion for `toSpecZ` (Nitsure §1, "Properness") is
1542	the genuine geometric content. Its first step (E1) is the chart-selection factorization:
1543	a `K`-point of `Gr(d,r)` for a field `K` factors through a single affine chart
1544	`U^I = Spec R^I`. Since `Spec K` is a single point and the chart immersions jointly cover
1545	`Gr(d,r)`, the image point lies in the range of some chart immersion, and a morphism out of
1546	`Spec K` whose image lies in the range of an open immersion factors through it
1547	(`IsOpenImmersion.lift`); affineness of the chart turns the lift into `Spec` of a ring map
1548	(`Spec.preimage`).
1549	
1550	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization`
1551	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1552	
1553	/-- **E1 — the `K`-point factors through a single chart**
1554	(`lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization`): for a field `K`, any morphism
1555	`i₁ : Spec K ⟶ Gr(d,r)` factors as `Spec(f) ≫ ι_I` through a single chart immersion `ι_I` of
1556	the glue datum (`theGlueData`), for some size-`d` subset `I` and ring homomorphism
1557	`f : R^I = ℤ[X^I] → K`. Project-local: step E1 of the valuative-criterion existence argument
1558	(Nitsure §1, "Properness"). -/
1559	theorem existence_chart_factorization (d r : ℕ) {K : Type} [Field K]
1560	    (i₁ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ scheme d r) :
1561	    ∃ (I : (theGlueData d r).J)
1562	      (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ →+* K),
1563	      i₁ = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f) ≫ (theGlueData d r).ι I := by
1564	  obtain ⟨x₀⟩ : Nonempty (↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of K))) := inferInstance
1565	  obtain ⟨I, y, hy⟩ := (theGlueData d r).ι_jointly_surjective (i₁.base x₀)
1566	  have hrange : Set.range i₁.base ⊆ Set.range ((theGlueData d r).ι I).base := by
1567	    rintro z ⟨x', rfl⟩
1568	    rw [Subsingleton.elim x' x₀]
1569	    exact ⟨y, hy⟩
1570	  haveI hoi : IsOpenImmersion ((theGlueData d r).ι I) := (theGlueData d r).ι_isOpenImmersion I
1571	  refine ⟨I, (Spec.preimage
1572	    (@IsOpenImmersion.lift _ _ _ ((theGlueData d r).ι I) i₁ hoi hrange)).hom, ?_⟩
1573	  rw [CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, Spec.map_preimage]
1574	  exact (@IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _ ((theGlueData d r).ι I) i₁ hoi hrange).symm
1575	
1576	/-- **E2 — minimal-valuation chart selection** (`lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation`):
1577	given a valuation ring `R` with fraction field `K`, valuation `v := ValuationRing.valuation R K`,
1578	a chart index `I` and a ring hom `f : R^I → K`, there is a chart index `J` *maximising*
1579	`v (f (P^I_J))` over the finite index set of size-`d` subsets, and at the maximiser
1580	`f (P^I_J) ≠ 0` (so the matrix `f(X^I_J)` is invertible over `K`). Since `P^I_I = 1`
1581	(`minorDet_self`) the value at `I` is `v 1 = 1`, so the maximum is `≥ 1 > 0`. Project-local:
1582	step E2 of the valuative-criterion existence argument (Nitsure §1, "Properness"). -/
1583	theorem existence_minimal_valuation (d r : ℕ)
1584	    {R K : Type} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] [ValuationRing R] [Field K]
1585	    [Algebra R K] [IsFractionRing R K]
1586	    (I : (theGlueData d r).J)
1587	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ →+* K) :
1588	    ∃ J : (theGlueData d r).J,
1589	      (∀ J' : (theGlueData d r).J,
1590	        ValuationRing.valuation R K (f (minorDet d r I.1 J'.1 I.2 J'.2))
1591	          ≤ ValuationRing.valuation R K (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)))
1592	      ∧ f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) ≠ 0 := by
1593	  haveI : Nonempty (theGlueData d r).J := ⟨I⟩
1594	  haveI : Finite (theGlueData d r).J :=
1595	    inferInstanceAs (Finite {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d})
1596	  set v := ValuationRing.valuation R K with hv
1597	  obtain ⟨J, hJmax⟩ := Finite.exists_max
1598	    (fun J' : (theGlueData d r).J => v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J'.1 I.2 J'.2)))
1599	  refine ⟨J, hJmax, ?_⟩
1600	  have h1 : v (f (minorDet d r I.1 I.1 I.2 I.2)) = 1 := by
1601	    rw [minorDet_self d r I.1 I.2, map_one, Valuation.map_one]
1602	  have hle : (1 : ValuationRing.ValueGroup R K) ≤ v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) := by
1603	    have := hJmax I; rwa [h1] at this
1604	  have hne : v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) ≠ 0 := by
1605	    intro h; rw [h] at hle; exact (not_le.mpr zero_lt_one) hle
1606	  exact (Valuation.ne_zero_iff v).mp hne
1607	
1608	/-- **E3 ratio core — the pulled-back minor-ratio identity**
1609	(displayed equation of `lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`): for a ring hom
1610	`f : R^I → F` under which the minor `P^I_J` is a unit, let `f' : R^I_J → F` be its
1611	away-localisation lift (`IsLocalization.Away.lift`). Then `g := f' ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` satisfies, for
1612	every third subset `K`,
1613	`g(P^J_K) · f(P^I_J) = f(P^I_K)`, i.e. `g(P^J_K) = f(P^I_K) / f(P^I_J)`.
1614	This is `f'` applied to the ring-level minor-ratio identity `transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul`.
1615	Project-local: the algebraic core of step E3; together with the valuation bound from E2 it
1616	drives the entries of `g(X^J)` into the valuation ring. (The remaining step — that every free
1617	entry `x^J_{p,q}` is, up to sign, such a minor `P^J_K` via cofactor expansion of a
1618	column-substituted identity — is the one matrix-algebra gap still open for E3.) -/
1619	theorem existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r))
1620	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) {F : Type} [CommRing F]
1621	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ →+* F)
1622	    (hf : IsUnit (f (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) :
1623	    (IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) hf)
1624	        (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))
1625	        * f (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
1626	      = f (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by
1627	  have h := congrArg (IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) hf)
1628	    (transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1629	  rwa [map_mul, IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq] at h
1630	
1631	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

892	
893	The key structural facts, all holding *definitionally* at the pinned commit, are:
894	* `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor` (`rfl`): the inverse leg
895	  of the site equivalence, post-composed with the slice-forgetful functor, is the opens functor of
896	  the open immersion `U.ι`;
897	* consequently `U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf = (overEquivalence_sheafCongr U RingCat).functor.obj
898	  (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (`rfl`): the structure sheaf of the open subscheme is the transport of
899	  the sliced structure sheaf across the ring-valued sheaf equivalence (this is *step 2*, the
900	  geometric ring-sheaf identification, discharged by `rfl`).
901	
902	From these, `SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` lifts the site equivalence to an
903	equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules (`overRestrictEquiv`, *step 3*), under whose functor
904	the sliced module `M.over U` corresponds to the geometric restriction (`overRestrictIso`, *step 4*).
905	
906	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies only the underlying site equivalence and the
907	`pushforward`/`restrictFunctor` machinery; the assembly is project-local. -/
908	
909	section OverRestrictBridge
910	
911	open TopologicalSpace Topology
912	
913	namespace Scheme.Modules
914	
915	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)
916	
917	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence of categories of sheaves of modules** (gap1, C, step 3).
918	
919	For a scheme `X` and an open `U ⊆ X`, the category of sheaves of modules over the sliced structure
920	sheaf `O_X.over U` on the slice site `J.over U` is equivalent to the category `U.toScheme.Modules`
921	of sheaves of modules on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The equivalence is obtained by lifting the
922	topological site equivalence `Opens.overEquivalence U` (and its ring-valued sheaf congruence
923	`overEquivalence_sheafCongr`) to sheaves of modules via
924	`SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`; the two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms it
925	consumes are the (co)unit of `Opens.overEquivalence U` whiskered into the structure presheaf, and
926	the identity (the geometric structure sheaf being *definitionally* the transport of the sliced one).
927	
928	Project-local: it is the module-level layer of the gap1 bridge `lem:over_restrict_iso`; Mathlib
929	supplies only the underlying site equivalence. -/
930	noncomputable def overRestrictEquiv :
931	    SheafOfModules.{u} (X.ringCatSheaf.over U) ≌ U.toScheme.Modules :=
932	  letI eqv := Opens.overEquivalence U
933	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
934	      (J := (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U) (K := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) eqv
935	    (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (R := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)
936	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op eqv.unitIso.inv) (X.ringCatSheaf.over U).obj))
937	    (Sheaf.Hom.mk (𝟙 _))
938	    (by ext : 2
939	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.whiskerLeft_app,
940	          Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app, NatTrans.id_app,
941	          ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app]
942	        exact congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map
943	          (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Equivalence.unitInv_app_inverse eqv _).symm))
944	    (by ext : 2
945	        simp only [Sheaf.Hom.mk, Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app,
946	          NatTrans.op_app, ObjectProperty.homMk_hom, NatTrans.comp_app, NatTrans.id_app,
947	          Functor.comp_obj]
948	        erw [Category.id_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
949	        rename_i x
950	        have h : (eqv.unitIso.inv.app (Opposite.unop x)).op ≫ (eqv.unit.app (Opposite.unop x)).op
951	            = 𝟙 _ := by
952	          rw [← op_comp]
953	          simp
954	        exact (congrArg (Sheaf.over X.ringCatSheaf U).obj.map h).trans
955	          (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id _ _))).symm
956	
957	/-- **Step-4 functor identification of the gap1 bridge.** The composite of `SheafOfModules.over · U`
958	with the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is the geometric restriction functor
959	`restrictFunctor U.ι` along the open immersion `U.ι`. Both are pushforwards along the immersion's
960	opens functor (`(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U = U.ι.opensFunctor`, `rfl`); the
961	two ring-sheaf comparison morphisms agree, so the identification is `pushforwardComp` followed by
962	`pushforwardCongr`. Project-local. -/
963	noncomputable def overRestrictFunctorIso :
964	    (SheafOfModules.pushforward (S := X.ringCatSheaf.over U) (𝟙 _)) ⋙
965	        (overRestrictEquiv U).functor ≅ restrictFunctor U.ι :=
966	  haveI : ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse ⋙ Over.forget U).IsContinuous
967	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X) :=
968	    Functor.isContinuous_comp (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse (Over.forget U)
969	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X).over U)
970	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥X)
971	  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _ ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)
972	
973	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism on an object** (gap1, C, step 4): for a sheaf of modules
974	`M` on `X`, the transport of the abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under the
975	slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically isomorphic to the geometric
976	restriction `(restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M`. This is the object-level form of
977	`overRestrictFunctorIso`; composed with `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` it lands the geometric
978	restriction as the pullback `U.ι^* M`. Project-local: the load-bearing slice-touching ingredient of
979	the gap1 transport `lem:over_restrict_iso`. -/
980	noncomputable def overRestrictIso (M : X.Modules) :
981	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (restrictFunctor U.ι).obj M :=
982	  (overRestrictFunctorIso U).app M
983	
984	/-- **The slice-to-geometric isomorphism in pullback form** (gap1, C, step 4'): the transport of the
985	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` under `overRestrictEquiv U` is canonically
986	isomorphic to the inverse-image (pullback) `U.ι^* M` of `M` along the open immersion `U.ι`. This is
987	`overRestrictIso` composed with Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`; it is the form a
988	presentation of `M.over U` transports into a presentation of the geometric pullback `U.ι^* M`.
989	Project-local. -/
990	noncomputable def overRestrictPullbackIso (M : X.Modules) :
991	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (M.over U) ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M :=
992	  overRestrictIso U M ≪≫ (restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).app M
993	
994	end Scheme.Modules
995	
996	end OverRestrictBridge

998	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — slice-to-geometric presentation transport (gap1, P1)
999	
1000	This section builds the geometric milestone of the gap1 per-element transport
1001	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1002	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U` is transported, across the slice-to-geometric
1003	bridge `overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C), into a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the *geometric*
1004	restriction `(Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`.
1005	
1006	The load-bearing ingredient is the unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso`: the slice-to-geometric
1007	equivalence functor `(overRestrictEquiv U).functor` (definitionally a `SheafOfModules.pushforward`
1008	along the equivalence-of-sites inverse with the *identity* ring comparison) sends the
1009	structure-sheaf module `unit` to `unit`. This is exactly the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum that
1010	`SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` consumes; once it is in hand, `Presentation.map` +
1011	`Presentation.ofIsIso` (across `overRestrictPullbackIso`) realise the transport. The unit-iso rests
1012	on the general fact `isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso'`: the canonical map
1013	`unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an iso whenever the ring-sheaf comparison `ψ` is
1014	(here `ψ = 𝟙`).
1015	
1016	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) supplies `SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit` and proves it iso
1017	only under a finality hypothesis on the site functor (`PullbackFree.lean`); the
1018	`IsIso ψ ⟹ IsIso (unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ)` route used here, and the slice transport, are
1019	project-local. -/
1020	
1021	section SliceGeometricPresentation
1022	
1023	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1024	
1025	/-- **`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` is an isomorphism when the ring-sheaf comparison is.**
1026	
1027	For a continuous functor `F` of sites and a morphism of ring sheaves
1028	`ψ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous …).obj R` that is an isomorphism, the canonical map
1029	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ : unit S ⟶ (pushforward ψ).obj (unit R)` is an isomorphism. Its
1030	component on each object is `(forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app _)`, iso as `ψ` is;
1031	the conclusion follows by the reflect-isomorphism functors `SheafOfModules.toSheaf` and
1032	`sheafToPresheaf` together with `NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app`.
1033	
1034	Project-local: Mathlib proves `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` iso only under a finality hypothesis on `F`
1035	(`SheafOfModules.PullbackFree`); this `IsIso ψ`-driven form is the one the slice-to-geometric
1036	unit-iso `overRestrictUnitIso` (gap1, P1) consumes (with `ψ = 𝟙`). -/
1037	private theorem isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C]
1038	    {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1039	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {Fu : C ⥤ D}
1040	    {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {Rr : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}} [Fu.IsContinuous J K]
1041	    (ψ : S ⟶ (Fu.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj Rr)
1042	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1043	    [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
1044	    (hψ : IsIso ψ) :
1045	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit ψ) := by
1046	  haveI := hψ
1047	  haveI hmap : IsIso ((sheafToPresheaf J RingCat).map ψ) := inferInstance
1048	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app] at hmap
1049	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf _)]
1050	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (sheafToPresheaf _ _)]
1051	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
1052	  intro V
1053	  haveI hiso : IsIso (ψ.hom.app V) := hmap V
1054	  haveI : IsIso ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (ψ.hom.app V)) := inferInstance
1055	  convert this using 1
1056	
1057	namespace Scheme.Modules
1058	
1059	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1060	
1061	/-- **The slice-to-geometric equivalence functor sends `unit` to `unit`** (gap1, P1).
1062	
1063	For an open `U ⊆ X`, the functor of the slice-to-geometric equivalence `overRestrictEquiv U`
1064	(definitionally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse` with the
1065	identity ring comparison) carries the sliced structure-sheaf module `unit (O_X.over U)` to the
1066	structure-sheaf module `unit (U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf)` of the open subscheme. This is the
1067	`F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum consumed by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` in
1068	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local. -/
1069	noncomputable def overRestrictUnitIso (U : X.Opens) :
1070	    (overRestrictEquiv U).functor.obj (SheafOfModules.unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)) ≅
1071	      SheafOfModules.unit U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf := by
1072	  unfold overRestrictEquiv
1073	  dsimp only [Equivalence.symm_functor]
1074	  refine (@asIso _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit
1075	      (F := (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse) (J := Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
1076	      (S := U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (R := X.ringCatSheaf.over U)
1077	      (ObjectProperty.homMk (𝟙 _)))
1078	    (isIso_unitToPushforwardObjUnit_of_isIso' _ ?hpsi)).symm
1079	  exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (𝟙 U.toScheme.ringCatSheaf))
1080	
1081	/-- **Slice presentation ⟹ geometric-restriction presentation** (gap1, P1).
1082	
1083	Given a sheaf of modules `M` on `X`, an open `U ⊆ X`, and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1084	abstract Grothendieck-slice restriction `M.over U`, there is a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of the
1085	*geometric* restriction `(pullback U.ι).obj M = U.ι^* M` on the open subscheme `U.toScheme`. The
1086	transport is `Presentation.map` along the slice-to-geometric equivalence functor (using the unit-iso
1087	`overRestrictUnitIso`) followed by `Presentation.ofIsIso` across the bridge
1088	`overRestrictPullbackIso` (gap1, C).
1089	
1090	This closes the slice-touching step of the gap1 per-element transport
1091	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): with `U = q.X i` and
1092	`P = q.presentation i` it produces a global presentation of `U.ι^* M`; the remaining geometric step
1093	restricts further to a basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1094	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. Project-local. -/
1095	noncomputable def overRestrictPresentation (U : X.Opens) (M : X.Modules)
1096	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).obj M).Presentation :=
1097	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.ofIsIso.{u} (overRestrictPullbackIso U M).hom
1098	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (overRestrictEquiv U).functor (overRestrictUnitIso U))
1099	
1100	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1101	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1103	/-- **Geometric restriction to a cover member is globally presented** (gap1, P1).
1104	
1105	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q` and an index `i`, the geometric
1106	restriction `(pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M = (q.X i).ι^* M` of `M` to the open subscheme
1107	`(q.X i).toScheme` admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation`. It is `overRestrictPresentation` applied
1108	to the slice presentation `q.presentation i : (M.over (q.X i)).Presentation` supplied by the
1109	quasi-coherence datum.
1110	
1111	This is the per-cover-member output that feeds the affine descent of the gap1 transport
1112	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent` (P1): for `D(r) ≤ q.X i` one further restricts this
1113	presentation to the basic affine `D(r) ≅ Spec R_r` and concludes via
1114	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`. The heartbeat headroom tames the slice-site
1115	`IsRightAdjoint`/`HasSheafify` synthesis blow-up that `Presentation.map` triggers across the
1116	equivalence functor (the same `backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` incantation Mathlib's own
1117	`QuasicoherentData.bind` uses). Project-local. -/
1118	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData (M : X.Modules)
1119	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) :
1120	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M).Presentation :=
1121	  overRestrictPresentation (q.X i) M (q.presentation i)
1122	
1123	end Scheme.Modules
1124	
1125	end SliceGeometricPresentation

1127	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — basic-open presentation descent (gap1, P1 keystone)
1128	
1129	This section assembles the gap1 per-element keystone
1130	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`
1131	(`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`): on a basic open `D(r)` contained in a cover member
1132	`q.X i` of the quasi-coherence data, the restricted sheaf `M|_{D(r)}` is a geometric tilde, i.e.
1133	its `fromTildeΓ` counit is an isomorphism.
1134	
1135	The route follows the affine descent of the recipe, building on the slice-to-geometric presentation
1136	transport of the previous section:
1137	
1138	1. `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` is a *global* `Presentation` of the geometric
1139	   restriction `N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on the open subscheme `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`.
1140	2. For any open `W ⊆ Z`, the global presentation `PN` slices to a slice presentation `N.over W` via
1141	   the single `Presentation.map` of the over-functor `pushforward (𝟙 …)` (the
1142	   `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template — no iterated-slice equivalence is needed because
1143	   `PN` is already a global presentation on the genuine scheme `Z`).
1144	3. `overRestrictPresentation W N PNW` transports it to a global presentation of the geometric
1145	   restriction `(pullback W.ι).obj N` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1146	4. For `W` *affine*, `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` identifies `W.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)`; transporting the
1147	   presentation across this iso (whose `Opens.map` is `Final`, so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso)
1148	   lands a global presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`.
1149	5. A global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an isomorphism
1150	   (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`).
1151	
1152	Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge; this descent
1153	is project-local. -/
1154	
1155	section BasicOpenPresentationDescent
1156	
1157	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace Topology
1158	
1159	namespace Scheme.Modules
1160	
1161	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1162	
1163	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1164	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1165	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1166	/-- **Presentation of the geometric restriction of `M` to an open `W` of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1167	
1168	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and *any* open
1169	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction
1170	`(pullback W.ι).obj ((pullback (q.X i).ι).obj M)` of `M` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`,
1171	then to `W`) admits a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` on the open subscheme `W.toScheme`.
1172	
1173	It slices the *global* presentation `presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i` of
1174	`N := (q.X i).ι^* M` on `Z` down to the slice `N.over W` (a single `Presentation.map` of the
1175	over-functor — the `QuasicoherentData.ofPresentation` template, no iterated-slice equivalence
1176	needed since `PN` is global on the genuine scheme `Z`), then geometrizes via
1177	`overRestrictPresentation`. Project-local: feeds the affine descent of the gap1 keystone
1178	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen`. -/
1179	noncomputable def presentationPullbackιRestrict (M : X.Modules)
1180	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I) (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) :
1181	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1182	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)).Presentation :=
1183	  overRestrictPresentation W ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)
1184	    (SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u}
1185	      (presentationPullbackιOfQuasicoherentData M q i)
1186	      (SheafOfModules.pushforward
1187	        (𝟙 ((show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf.over W))) (by rfl))
1188	
1189	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is an equivalence of opens sites.** For `φ : Y ≅ Z`,
1190	the inverse-image functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base : Opens ↥Y ⥤ Opens ↥Z` is an equivalence (with
1191	inverse `Opens.map φ.hom.base`), assembled from the pseudofunctoriality isos `Opens.mapComp` /
1192	`Opens.mapId`. Its purpose is to supply the `Final` instance that makes `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an
1193	isomorphism in `pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso`. Project-local. -/
1194	noncomputable def opensMapEquivOfIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1195	    TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y ≌ TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Z where
1196	  functor := Opens.map φ.inv.base
1197	  inverse := Opens.map φ.hom.base
1198	  unitIso := (Opens.mapId _).symm ≪≫
1199	      Opens.mapIso (𝟙 _) (φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base)
1200	        (show (𝟙 _) = φ.hom.base ≫ φ.inv.base by
1201	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.hom_inv_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1202	      Opens.mapComp φ.hom.base φ.inv.base
1203	  counitIso := (Opens.mapComp φ.inv.base φ.hom.base).symm ≪≫
1204	      Opens.mapIso (φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base) (𝟙 _)
1205	        (show φ.inv.base ≫ φ.hom.base = 𝟙 _ by
1206	          rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.comp_base, φ.inv_hom_id]; rfl) ≪≫
1207	      Opens.mapId _
1208	
1209	/-- **The opens functor of an iso of schemes is final.** Immediate from
1210	`opensMapEquivOfIso` (an equivalence is final); the `Final` fact needed by
1211	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`. Supplied via `haveI` at use sites (instance resolution cannot invert
1212	`φ.inv.base`). Project-local. -/
1213	theorem opensMap_final_of_schemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1214	    (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := by
1215	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).IsEquivalence := (opensMapEquivOfIso φ).isEquivalence_functor
1216	  infer_instance
1217	
1218	/-- **Pullback along an iso of schemes sends the unit module to the unit module** (gap1, P1).
1219	
1220	For an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z`, the pullback functor along `φ.inv : Z ⟶ Y` carries the
1221	structure-sheaf (unit) module of `Y` to that of `Z`. The underlying canonical comparison
1222	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an isomorphism because the site functor `Opens.map φ.inv.base` of an iso
1223	of schemes is `Final` (`opensMap_final_of_schemeIso`). This is the `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S` datum
1224	consumed by `Presentation.map` along `pullback φ.inv` in `presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`.
1225	Project-local. -/
1226	noncomputable def pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z) :
1227	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).obj (SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf) ≅
1228	      SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf := by
1229	  haveI : (Opens.map φ.inv.base).Final := opensMap_final_of_schemeIso φ
1230	  haveI : (SheafOfModules.pushforward (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom)).IsRightAdjoint := inferInstance
1231	  exact asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (φ.inv.toRingCatSheafHom))
1232	
1233	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1234	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1235	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1236	/-- **A presentation transports across the pullback by an iso of schemes** (gap1, P1, step 4).
1237	
1238	Given an isomorphism of schemes `φ : Y ≅ Z` and a `SheafOfModules.Presentation` of a module `N` on
1239	`Y`, the geometric pullback `(pullback φ.inv).obj N` of `N` to `Z` admits a presentation. It is
1240	`Presentation.map` along the colimit-preserving pullback functor `pullback φ.inv`, using the unit-iso
1241	`pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ`. This is the affine-identification transport step of the gap1 keystone:
1242	applied with `φ` the `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` of the affine restriction, it moves the presentation onto
1243	a genuine `Spec`. Project-local. -/
1244	noncomputable def presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso {Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (φ : Y ≅ Z)
1245	    (N : Y.Modules) (P : N.Presentation) :
1246	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv).obj N).Presentation :=
1247	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1} (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv) :=
1248	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ.inv).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
1249	  SheafOfModules.Presentation.map.{u} P (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ.inv)
1250	    (pullbackSchemeIsoUnitIso φ)
1251	
1252	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1253	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1254	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1255	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on every affine open of a cover member** (gap1, P1).
1256	
1257	For a sheaf of modules `M` on `X` with quasi-coherence data `q`, an index `i`, and an *affine* open
1258	`W ⊆ (q.X i).toScheme` of the cover-member subscheme, the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine
1259	`Spec Γ((q.X i).toScheme, W) ≅ W` (pulled back to `Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, then to `W`, then across
1260	the affine identification `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`) has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit — i.e. it is
1261	a geometric tilde.
1262	
1263	This is the geometric heart of the gap1 per-element transport: the slice presentation supplied by
1264	the quasi-coherence datum geometrizes (`presentationPullbackιRestrict`) to a global presentation on
1265	`W.toScheme`, which transports across the affine iso (`presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso`) to a global
1266	presentation on the genuine affine `Spec Γ(Z, W)`; a global presentation forces `fromTildeΓ` to be an
1267	isomorphism (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`). Project-local: Mathlib has no
1268	`QCoh(Spec R) ≃ Mod R` essential-image bridge. -/
1269	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback (M : X.Modules)
1270	    (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (i : q.I)
1271	    (W : (show X.Opens from q.X i).toScheme.Opens) (hW : IsAffineOpen W) :
1272	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hW.isoSpec.inv).obj
1273	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1274	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))).fromTildeΓ :=
1275	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation _
1276	    (presentationPullbackOfSchemeIso hW.isoSpec
1277	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι W)).obj
1278	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M))
1279	      (presentationPullbackιRestrict M q i W))
1280	
1281	set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
1282	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
1283	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1284	/-- **Quasi-coherent restricts to a tilde on each basic open of the cover** (gap1, P1 keystone,
1285	`lem:isIso_fromTildeΓ_basicOpen_of_quasicoherent`).
1286	
1287	Let `M` be a sheaf of modules on `Spec R` with quasi-coherence data `q`, and let `r : R` with
1288	`D(r) ≤ q.X i` for some cover member. Then the geometric restriction of `M` to the affine basic open
1289	`D(r)` — realised as the preimage `W := (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` inside the cover-member subscheme
1290	`Z := (q.X i).toScheme`, transported across the affine identification `W ≅ Spec Γ(Z, W)` (which is
1291	`Spec R_r` since `D(r)` is affine) — has an isomorphism `fromTildeΓ` counit, i.e. `M|_{D(r)}` is a
1292	geometric tilde.
1293	
1294	This is the per-element step of gap1: it is the affine instance `W = (q.X i).ι ⁻¹ᵁ D(r)` of
1295	`isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback`, with affineness of `W` from
1296	`IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen` (`D(r)` is affine in `Spec R`) and
1297	`IsAffineOpen.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion` (its preimage under the open immersion `(q.X i).ι` is
1298	affine, using `D(r) ≤ q.X i = (q.X i).ι.opensRange`). Project-local. -/
1299	theorem isIso_fromTildeΓ_restrict_basicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
1300	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (q : M.QuasicoherentData) (r : R) (i : q.I)
1301	    (hr : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ q.X i) :
1302	    IsIso (@Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
1303	      (Γ(↑(q.X i), (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))
1304	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
1305	          (((IsAffineOpen.Spec_basicOpen r).preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))
1306	            (by rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hr)).isoSpec.inv)).obj
1307	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι
1308	            ((Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i)) ⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)))).obj
1309	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (q.X i))).obj M)))) :=
1310	  isIso_fromTildeΓ_presentationPullback M q i

2251	`Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, `r' ⊗ m ↦ (r' ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); since `cancelBaseChange` carries no

2266	theorem pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange

2269	      (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
2270	        (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))

2272	        (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M))) := by
